title: Incremental parsing and changed range detection
description:
Implement incremental re-parsing so that edits to already-parsed files reuse unchanged subtrees.

Deliverables:
- Tree.Edit() with immutable tree / structural sharing: clone only nodes on the edit path (O(depth) = O(log N)). Propagate has_changes flag, adjust padding/size, handle depends_on_column invalidation.
- ReusableNode iterator: stack-based DFS over old tree with (tree, childIndex, byteOffset) entries. lastExternalToken tracking.
- Reusability checks in parser.advance(): has_changes, error node, missing node, fragile (fragileLeft/fragileRight), external scanner state match, included_range_difference overlap, first leaf lex mode compatibility, is_reusable table flag.
- changed_ranges.go: parallel tree walk for change detection. Two Iterator structs walking old and new trees. Compare by symbol, size, parse state, error cost, external scanner state. Merge adjacent changed ranges.
- Tree balancing: subtree.compress() with AVL-like rotations on repetition nodes (tracked by repeatDepth). Keep depth O(log N) for left-recursive repetitions.
- Structural hashing (Alien Artifact AA-2): compute bottom-up structural hashes during tree construction. Tree.ChangedRanges() first compares root hashes — if equal, return nil in O(1), avoiding O(N) tree walk. This is low-hanging fruit (O(1) extra work per node during construction) that provides 10-100x speedup for whitespace-only edits.
- Arena lifetime management: new tree holds references to both its own arena and old tree's arena(s) for shared subtrees.

Tests:
- Edit-and-reparse cycles: single char insert/delete, line insert/delete, multi-line edit
- Verify incremental parse produces identical tree to from-scratch parse
- Benchmark incremental vs full parse (expect >99% subtree reuse for single-char edits)
- ChangedRanges correctness (ranges match expectations for various edit patterns)
- Structural hash fast path: verify whitespace-only edits return empty ChangedRanges in O(1)
- Tree balancing: parse file with 1000+ top-level statements, verify balanced depth
- Column-dependent invalidation (Python-style indentation scenarios)
- Arena lifetime: verify old tree's subtrees remain valid after old tree is discarded but new tree is retained

Reference: docs/design.md §6, docs/addendum-incremental-parsing.md §1-2, §3b (structural hashing), C source: subtree.c (ts_subtree_edit), parser.c (ts_parser__reuse_node), get_changed_ranges.c, reusable_node.h
